skill.name: firebase-hosting-basics
skill.description: Guidance for configuring and deploying Firebase Hosting Classic sites, preview channels, redirects, rewrites, headers, and local emulation.
skill.summary: Configure, test, preview, and deploy Firebase Hosting Classic projects with Firebase CLI guidance.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backtick and weak-crypto patterns, but review found these are markdown code spans, JSON examples, and Firebase Hosting terminology rather than executable malware. The real risk is legitimate Firebase CLI guidance that can install tooling, start a local server, deploy Hosting content, and promote preview channels, so users should require confirmation before running commands.

medium_findings.title: Firebase CLI Commands Can Change Hosted Sites
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends Firebase CLI commands for deployment, preview-channel deployment, expiration changes, and cloning a channel to live. These commands are legitimate for Firebase Hosting but can publish content or promote releases if an agent runs them without explicit user approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly documented Firebase deploy and channel-management operations. The risk depends on whether an agent executes them without user confirmation, so this is an operational risk rather than malicious code.

medium_findings.title: Latest Package Execution Requires User Control
medium_findings.description: The skill suggests running firebase-tools through npx with the latest version. This is common Firebase guidance, but it downloads and executes package code from the npm ecosystem and should be run only in a trusted project context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The npx commands are explicit and would invoke external tooling. No malicious package source or obfuscation is present, but package execution is a real supply-chain and change-control concern.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks and JSON Examples
low_findings.description: Most external-command alerts point to markdown inline code, fenced JSON, and Firebase configuration examples. These are documentation snippets, not Ruby shell backtick execution, and no code path executes them automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is plain markdown documentation and JSON configuration. I found no Ruby code, shell backtick expansion, or automatic execution mechanism in these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography alerts occur in ordinary Firebase Hosting prose and JSON destinations. No cryptographic algorithm selection, hash function call, or encryption implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain descriptions of static hosting or route destinations, not cryptographic primitives. The scanner appears to have matched unrelated terms inside documentation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Localhost URL Reference
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL alert is a local emulator address used to explain where Firebase Hosting serves content during local testing. It is not an external exfiltration endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL uses localhost and is described as the default local emulator address. I found no code that sends data to this address.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Deployment Commands in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell commands that can deploy Firebase Hosting content, create preview channels, and clone a preview release to live. These should be treated as user-approved operational commands, not passive documentation, when used by an agent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is visible and operationally meaningful, but it is expected for a hosting deployment skill. The concern is uncontrolled execution, not malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Firebase Hosting setup can be error-prone when projects need redirects, rewrites, previews, and releases. This skill gives focused Firebase Hosting Classic guidance for configuration, local testing, and deployment workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Firebase Hosting Classic instead of Firebase App Hosting.
Guides firebase.json setup for public directories, ignores, redirects, rewrites, headers, clean URLs, and trailing slashes.
Provides Firebase CLI commands for local Hosting emulation.
Provides deployment commands for live Hosting releases and preview channels.
Explains how to clone a preview channel release to the live channel.
limitations: Does not cover Firebase App Hosting setup for full-stack frameworks.
Does not generate complete application source code or build pipelines.
Does not manage Firebase authentication, billing, or project permissions.
Requires users to verify project IDs, site IDs, and channel names before deployment.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Static Site Release
use_cases.description: Configure Firebase Hosting Classic for a static site with a public directory, ignored files, clean URLs, and cache-related headers.

use_cases.title: Validate SPA Routing
use_cases.description: Set up rewrites so a single-page application serves index.html for client-side routes while preserving API paths.

use_cases.title: Promote a Tested Preview
use_cases.description: Use preview channels to review changes, set expiration timing, and promote a tested channel to live hosting.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the Hosting Product
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a web project with these traits: [describe framework, static output, SSR needs, and deploy workflow]. Tell me whether Firebase Hosting Classic or Firebase App Hosting is the better fit.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Basic Hosting Configuration
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a simple firebase.json for a static site.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Firebase Hosting Classic configuration for a site built into [public directory]. Include sensible ignores, clean URLs, and trailing slash behavior. Explain each setting briefly.

prompt_templates.title: Configure SPA and API Rewrites
prompt_templates.scenario: Support client-side routes and a backend endpoint.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this hosting goal: [describe SPA routes and API path]. Propose Firebase Hosting rewrites for index.html and the API backend. Include any ordering concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Preview-to-Live Release
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced release workflow with preview channels.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Firebase Hosting Classic release checklist for preview channel [channel], site [site ID], and live promotion. Include commands, validation steps, and approval points before live deployment.

output_examples.input: A React SPA builds to dist and needs all client routes to load index.html.
output_examples.output: A concise firebase.json plan that sets dist as the public directory, preserves default ignores, and adds a catch-all rewrite to index.html for SPA routing.

output_examples.input: A team wants to test a feature branch before changing the live Firebase Hosting site.
output_examples.output: A preview-channel workflow with a named channel, an expiration choice, a review URL expectation, and a separate promotion step for the live channel.

output_examples.input: A static site needs cleaner URLs and font files served with cross-origin access.
output_examples.output: A hosting configuration outline that enables clean URLs and adds a targeted response header rule for font assets.

best_practices: Confirm the Firebase project and site before running any deploy or clone command.
Use preview channels for review before promoting changes to the live channel.
Keep Hosting rewrites ordered so specific API or container routes are evaluated before catch-all SPA routes.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for Firebase App Hosting or SSR-first framework deployment guidance.
Do not run deploy commands from an unreviewed working tree or an unexpected Firebase project.
Do not place broad catch-all rewrites before more specific backend routes.

faq.question: Is this skill for Firebase Hosting Classic or Firebase App Hosting?
faq.answer: It is for Firebase Hosting Classic. Use Firebase App Hosting for supported full-stack frameworks that need SSR or automated git-based deployment.

faq.question: Can it help with single-page applications?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains catch-all rewrites that serve index.html so client-side routes load correctly.

faq.question: Does it deploy code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides commands and guidance. Users should approve commands and verify Firebase project context before execution.

faq.question: Does it cover preview channels?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes preview channel deployment, expiration options, and cloning a preview release to live.

faq.question: Can it configure headers and redirects?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes Firebase Hosting configuration for redirects, response headers, clean URLs, trailing slashes, and rewrites.

faq.question: Does it manage Firebase permissions or billing?
faq.answer: No. It does not manage IAM, billing, authentication, or project ownership settings.
